sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the initial state more clearly:
- N(W) at cols 21-23 (left)
- White square at cols 24-26 (middle-left)
- N(B) at cols 27-29 (middle-right)
- White square at cols 30-32 (right)
- Purple wall at cols 33-38
So initially there are TWO N tokens (N(W) and N(B)) and TWO white squares between them and the wall. The N tokens can move through the white squares.
I moved N(W) right through the white squares to the wall. Now I need to figure out what to do.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. The containers are:
- Left container: at rows 32-36, cols 11-15, with white interior
- Right container: at rows 26-30, cols 44-48, with white interior
The left container is BELOW the N tokens' horizontal path. Maybe I need to move N(W) DOWN into the left container. Let me try that.
But wait, I already moved N(W) all the way to the right, next to the purple wall. I need to move it back left and then down into the left container.
Actually, let me reconsider. The left container is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The N tokens are at rows 30-32. So the container is below and to the left of the N tokens.
Let me try moving N(W) LEFT to get back, then DOWN into the left container.
But wait - I've already spent 18 actions. Let me think about what the most efficient path forward is. Maybe I should try to move N(W) down from its current position (rows 30-32, cols 30-32) to see if it can reach the left container.
Actually, the left container is at cols 11-15, rows 32-36. N(W) is at cols 30-32, rows 30-32. To get to the container, I'd need to move LEFT many times and then DOWN. That's a lot of actions.
Let me reconsider the puzzle entirely. Maybe the goal isn't to put N tokens into containers. Maybe the containers are already "filled" with white interiors, and the goal is something else.
Wait, looking at the initial state again:
- Left container has white interior (w)
- Right container has white interior (w)
And the N tokens have:
- N(W) with white center (W)
- N(B) with black center (B)
Maybe the goal is to match colors: N(W) goes to a white container, N(B) goes to a black container. But both containers are white...
Or maybe the containers need to be "activated" by clicking on them, and the N tokens need to be moved to specific positions.
Let me try a different approach. Let me look at the state graph to see if there are any states that look like a "solved" state (e.g., a state with a different score or completed flag).
Actually, let me try something simpler: maybe the goal is just to move both N tokens into their respective containers. The left container is on the left side, and the right container is on the right side. N(W) starts on the left, and N(B) starts on the right (relative to each other). So maybe N(W) goes to the left container and N(B) goes to the right container.
But N(B) is on the left side of the purple wall, and the right container is on the right side. So N(B) can't reach the right container.
Unless... the purple wall can be removed or passed through somehow. Let me try clicking on the purple wall.
